metadata.title: Why the Centrifugal Barrier Is Angular Kinetic Energy

beats.narration_text: Namaste. Liam, in for Bear. The centrifugal barrier looks like a repulsive potential in the radial Schrodinger equation. Its origin, however, is angular kinetic energy, not a new interaction force.

props.command: Trace the centrifugal barrier back to its kinetic-energy origin.
props.segment: Why the Centrifugal Barrier Is Angular Kinetic Energy
props.runningText: separating radial and angular kinetic energy…
output: The term originates in L-squared over two mu r-squared.
In a fixed-ell radial equation it acts as an effective potential.

beats.narration_text: After separating variables, the radial equation contains V of r plus h-bar squared ell times ell plus one over two mu r squared. For ell zero, this centrifugal term vanishes.

For nonzero ell, the positive one-over-r-squared term diverges near the origin and suppresses radial probability there. That statement is safer than claiming every higher-ell state has a larger mean radius, which also depends on n and the potential.
The term behaves like repulsion and is grouped with the effective potential. Yet it came from the kinetic operator. Is it kinetic energy, potential energy, or a force?
Its placement tempts us to call it a new repulsive force. But moving terms across an equation or grouping them into an effective potential does not change their physical origin.
The three-dimensional kinetic operator separates into radial kinetic energy and angular kinetic energy. The angular piece is L squared over two mu r squared.
On an angular-momentum eigenstate, L squared becomes h-bar squared ell times ell plus one. The operator therefore becomes a positive r-dependent number in the reduced radial problem.

It is then convenient and mathematically legitimate to include that number in V effective. So it is an effective-potential term with a kinetic origin, not a fundamental interaction potential produced by another field or particle.

Increasing ell raises the one-over-r-squared barrier and more strongly excludes the wavefunction from very small r. It does not by itself determine a universal orbital radius ordering across different states.
When drawing V effective for ell one, label the positive term centrifugal barrier or angular kinetic contribution. Do not mistake its effective radial repulsion for a new fundamental force.

Angular kinetic energy becomes h-bar squared ell times ell plus one over two mu r squared. In the fixed-ell radial equation we group it into an effective potential. Kinetic origin, effective-potential role, no new interaction.
Your turn. Compare ell zero and ell two. The centrifugal coefficient ell times ell plus one is zero for ell zero and six for ell two. Which state is more suppressed near the origin? Ell two.
Why the Centrifugal Barrier Is Angular Kinetic Energy.
